properties.title: Deploy AVD Session Hosts to an existing host pool

location.label: Session Host/VM Region
location.toolTip: Select the region to which the Session Hosts are to be deployed.

elements.name: hostPool
elements.label: Select the Host Pool to update
elements.toolTip: Select the AVD Hostpool.

elements.name: resourceGroupHosts
elements.label: Session Host/VM Resource Group
elements.toolTip: Select the name of the existing resource group where the session host virtual machines and associated resources will be created. This resouce group name should be similar to the host pool name and include the word hosts or compute.

elements.name: environmentShortName
elements.label: Environment
elements.toolTip: The environment is used in resource naming. only specify if naming production, develop, and test resources distinctly.

options.text: This solution requires that several artifacts are uploaded as blobs to an existing storage account.

Select the storage account and blob container with the build artifacts (scripts and installers) and optionally the User Assigned Managed Identity that has been assigned the 'Storage Blob Data Reader' role the storage account.

elements.name: storageAccount
elements.label: Image Artifacts Storage Account
elements.toolTip: Select the storage account containing the image customization artifacts (i.e., installers, scripts).

elements.name: container
elements.label: Image Artifacts Container
elements.toolTip: Select the container which contains the software and script blobs.

elements.name: managedIdentity
elements.label: Storage Account access User Assigned Identity
elements.toolTip: Select an existing User Assigned Identity which has been assigned data plane rights of at least 'Storage Blob Data Reader' to the storage account and container selected above. If you don't select an existing user assigned managed identity, then it will be created automatically.

elements.name: avdAgentBlob
elements.toolTip: Select the Session Host Configuration Blob. Must contain the AVD Agent and AVD Boot Loader Binary Files.'Set-SessionHostConfiguration.zip' is used by default.
elements.label: Session Host Configuration Script Blob (.zip)

elements.name: virtualMachineNamePrefix
elements.label: Custom VM Name Prefix
elements.toolTip: Provide a custom VM Name prefix consisting of alphanumeric and dashes up to 12 characters long. A three character numeric suffix will be appended to the name padded with zeros (0).

constraints.validationMessage: The prefix must begin with an alphanumeric character and then contain only alphanumeric characters and dashes. The value must also be less than 12 characters long.

elements.name: nameConvResTypeAtEnd
elements.label: Move the Resource Type to the end of resource names

elements.name: index
elements.label: Starting number (index)
elements.toolTip: Select the start number of the virtual machine suffix.

elements.name: virtualNetwork
elements.label: Virtual Network
elements.toolTip: Select the virtual network where the AVD Private Link Private Endpoints will be created.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('hosts').network.virtualNetworksApi.value, (item) => equals(item.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name)), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: subnet
elements.label: Subnet
elements.toolTip: Select an existing subnet for the AVD session hosts.

elements.name: securityType
elements.label: Security Type
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate Security Type configuration for the Virtual Machine. Note that 'Trusted Launch' and 'Confidential Virtual Machines' are not available in all regions and may require specific VM Sizes.

options.text: Confidential disk encryption is an additional layer of encryption which binds the disk encryption keys to the virtual machine's TPM and makes the disk content accessible only to the VM.

elements.name: keyManagement
elements.label: Key Management
elements.toolTip: Platform-managed keys are key encryption keys that are generated, stored, and managed entirely by Azure. Choose Platform Managed for the best balance of security and ease of use. Customer-managed keys are key encryption keys that are generated, stored, and managed by you, the customer, in your Azure Key Vault. Choose Customer Managed if you need to meet specific compliance requirements. Platform-Managed and Customer-Managed Keys provide double encryption with an infrastructure encryption layer using platform-managed keys and a disk encryption layer using customer-managed keys.

elements.name: diskEncryptionSet
elements.label: Disk Encryption Set (for Customer-Managed keys)
elements.toolTip: Select the appropriate Disk Encryption Set.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('hosts').security.diskEncryptionSetsApi.value, (item) => and(equals(item.location, steps('basics').scope.location.name), if(steps('hosts').security.confidentialVMOSDiskEncryption, contains(item.properties.encryptionType, 'Confidential'), if(contains(steps('hosts').security.keyManagement, 'Platform'), contains(item.properties.encryptionType, 'Platform'), equals(item.properties.encryptionType, 'EncryptionAtRestWithCustomerKey'))))), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: count
elements.label: Number of Virtual Machines
elements.toolTip: Select the number of virtual machines to deploy in your AVD host pool.

elements.name: size
elements.label: Virtual Machine Size
elements.toolTip: Select the size of the virtual machines. Multi-session hosts should have 4 - 24 vCPUs. Single session host should have 2 or more vCPUs.

constraints.allowedValues: [if(equals(steps('hosts').security.securityType, 'ConfidentialVM'), map(filter(steps('hosts').specs.vmSizesApi.value, (item) => and(or(startsWith(item.name, 'Standard_DC'), startsWith(item.name, 'Standard_EC')), endsWith(item.name, '5'))), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","value":"', item.name, '"}'))), map(steps('hosts').specs.vmSizesApi.value, (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","value":"', item.name, '"}'))))]

elements.name: diskSku
elements.label: OS Disk SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the disk SKU for the operating system disk.

elements.name: availability
elements.label: Availability Options
elements.toolTip: Select the redundancy / resiliency for the virtual machines.

elements.name: source
elements.label: Image Source
elements.toolTip: Select the type of image to deploy on the session hosts.

elements.name: offer
elements.label: Offer
elements.toolTip: Select the desired marketplace image offer.

elements.name: sku
elements.label: SKU
elements.toolTip: Select the desired marketplace image SKU.

elements.name: solution
elements.label: Identity Provider
elements.toolTip: Choose the Identity Solution for the Virtual Machines.

elements.name: intune
elements.label: Intune Enrollment
elements.toolTip: If Intune is configured in your Azure AD tenant, you can choose to have the VM automatically enrolled during the deployment by selecting Yes.

elements.name: domainName
elements.label: Domain Name
elements.toolTip: Provide domain name for the selected Active Directory solution.

elements.name: ouPath
elements.label: OU Path
elements.toolTip: Input the distinguished name of the desired organization unit for the AVD session hosts.

elements.name: userPrincipalName
elements.label: User Principal Name
elements.toolTip: Enter the user principal name with domain join privileges.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be a valid user principal name.

elements.name: password
elements.toolTip: Enter a password that is alphanumeric, contains at least 12 characters, 1 letter, 1 number and 1 special character.

elements.name: username
elements.label: Username
elements.toolTip: Input the username for the local administrator account.

elements.name: password
elements.toolTip: Input the password for the local administrator account.

constraints.validationMessage: The value must be within 12 to 123 characters, be alphanumeric, and include 1 lower case character, 1 upper case character, 1 number, and 1 special character that is not '\' or '-'.

elements.name: configureHosts
elements.label: Configure FSLogix Settings on Session Hosts

elements.name: fslogixBlob
elements.toolTip: Select FsLogix Configuration Script blob. 'Configure-FSLogix.zip' is used by default.
elements.label: FsLogix Configuration Script Blob (.zip)

elements.name: fslogixContainerType
elements.label: FSLogix Container Type(s)
elements.toolTip: Select the solution for the FSLogix profiles.

options.text: Select the Azure storage accounts used for FSLogix profile and office containers in the number and order of preference as defined by the in the note below.

For Entra Id authentication with cloud cache container(s), provide up to four (4) storage accounts, limited to one (1) per region because sharding is not possible. Configure them below in the order of preference with the storage account located in the region of the session hosts as the first listed.
For Entra Id authentication with VHD container(s), provide the one (1) storage account in the same region as the virtual machines because sharding is not possible.

elements.name: fslogixExistingStorageAccounts
elements.ariaLabel: FSLogix Existing Storage Account Resource Ids
elements.label: FSLogix Existing Storage Account Resource Ids

options.text: For the 'Entra Id' identity provider selected on the 'Virtual Machines' blade with standard profile or profile and office containers, you can specify a maximum of 1 storage account in the same region as the virtual machines.
Enter the blob names (case-sensitive) located in the storage account container chosen on the 'Basics' page.

elements.name: recoveryServices
elements.label: Enable Recovery Services
elements.toolTip: Choose to deploy backups for your solution. For a personal host pool, this will enable backups on the virtual machines. For a pooled host pool, this will enable backups on the FSLogix file share when using Azure Files.

elements.name: recoveryServicesVault
elements.label: Existing Recovery Services Vault for Virtual Machine Backups
elements.toolTip: Select the recovery services vault used for storing backups of the session hosts in this pool.

elements.name: enableInsights
elements.label: Enable Monitoring for AVD and VM Insights
elements.toolTip: Deploy the required resources to enable AVD and VM Insights.

elements.name: dataCollectionEndpoint
elements.label: Data Collection Endpoint
elements.toolTip: Select the Data Collection Endpoint used by the Azure Monitor Agent.

elements.name: avdInsightsDataCollectionRule
elements.label: AVD Insights Data Collection Rule
elements.toolTip: Select the data collection rule that will be associated with the session hosts.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('management').monitoring.dataCollectionRulesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'microsoft-avdi')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '","value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: vmInsightsDataCollectionRule
elements.label: Virtual Machine Insights Data Collection Rule
elements.toolTip: Select the data collection rule that will be associated with the session hosts.

constraints.allowedValues: [map(filter(steps('management').monitoring.dataCollectionRulesApi.value, (item) => contains(item.name, 'MSVMI-')), (item) => parse(concat('{"label":"', item.name, '","description":"Resource group: ', first(skip(split(item.id, '/'), 4)), '", "value":"', item.id, '"}')))]

elements.name: enableSecurity
elements.label: Enable Security Monitoring
elements.toolTip: Deploy the Log Analytics (Microsoft Monitoring) Agent or a Data Collection Rule Association to use the Azure Monitoring Agent for collecting security relevant data.

elements.name: securityDataCollectionRule
elements.label: Data Collection Rule for Security
elements.toolTip: Select the Data Collection Rule used for collecting security data for Sentinel or Defender for Cloud. This is required to create the Data Collection Rule Association

elements.name: logAnalyticsWorkspace
elements.label: Existing Log Analytics Workspace for Security
elements.toolTip: Select the log analytics workspace used for collecting security data for Sentinel or Defender for Cloud. This is required to deploy and configure the Microsoft Monitoring Agent.
